text:
$TAO : Review

Yesterday at 5:21 PM, the United States government ordered Anthropic to immediately shut down Fable 5 and Mythos 5 for every user on earth. Hundreds of millions of people lost access to the most powerful AI models commercially available because one government official sent one export control directive on a Friday evening.

Anthropic called it a "misunderstanding." The models went dark anyway. Thousands of businesses relying on the API face immediate disruption.
One directive. One company. One kill switch. The entire world's access to frontier AI, gone.

Now ask yourself: what if that AI ran on a network that no government, no corporation, and no single person could shut down?

Meet Bittensor - a decentralized blockchain network creating the "Internet of AI," where 120+ specialized subnets compete to produce the best machine intelligence and contributors earn TAO tokens for real informational value. Bitcoin tokenomics meets decentralized machine learning. Bitwise filed for spot ETFs. And unlike Anthropic's Fable 5, nobody can turn it off.

Let's explore why yesterday's shutdown proves that decentralized AI isn't optional. It's inevitable. 👇

⚪ Bittensor at a Glance

Bittensor is a decentralized blockchain network designed to create a global, open marketplace for artificial intelligence, where contributors are incentivized with its native TAO token. The network connects users who need AI services with a distributed network of providers contributing machine learning models and computational power.

The $TAO token has a fixed supply of 21 million (mirroring Bitcoin), with halving cycles and no premine. It functions as the fuel for the entire ecosystem: staking, network fees, governance, and accessing AI services.

Marketplace Insight: Yesterday the US government proved exactly why Bittensor exists. One directive shut down Anthropic's most powerful AI for the entire planet. Bittensor's 120+ subnets, running across thousands of nodes worldwide, have no kill switch. No CEO to receive a directive. No centralized server to disable. No single jurisdiction to comply with.

Nvidia CEO Jensen Huang endorsed Bittensor's decentralized AI training approach. Grayscale and Bitwise have pending spot TAO ETF applications with SEC decision expected August 2026. The emissions refactor (May 2026) concentrated rewards to the top 30 performing subnets. This isn't speculative AI hype. After yesterday, it's existential infrastructure.

⚪ Mission

Bittensor's mission is to prevent AI from being owned by the few. AI must not be owned by the few for the same, and exponentially more dangerous, threat that motivated Bitcoin to decentralize finance. Yesterday's Anthropic shutdown proved the thesis in real-time: when AI runs on centralized servers, governments have a kill switch. When AI runs on Bittensor, nobody does. By creating an open marketplace where machine intelligence is a tradeable commodity, Bittensor aims to make cutting-edge AI development accessible, transparent, and impossible to censor.

🔵 A Brief History

Bittensor was founded in 2019 by Jacob Robert Steeves and Ala Shaabana. Steeves previously worked as a machine learning engineer at Google's Google Brain team. Shaabana has a background as a Postdoctoral Fellow at the University of Waterloo and as an Assistant Professor at the University of Toronto.

The concept was conceived as early as 2015, designed in 2016, and the network went live on January 3, 2021. Together they published the Subtensor white paper, raised an $11M seed round led by Polychain Capital, and open-sourced the entire codebase under an Apache 2.0 license.

The blockchain layer (Subtensor) launched in March 2023 on Substrate. In February 2025, the dTAO upgrade transformed token issuance from validator-controlled allocation to pure market forces, with each subnet receiving its own alpha token tradeable against TAO via an AMM.
On March 10, 2026, Subnet 3 (Templar) completed the training of Covenant-72B, a 72-billion parameter language model trained in a decentralized manner across 70+ nodes worldwide. It outperformed Meta's LLaMA-2-70B on the MMLU benchmark.

Analysts described it as "Bittensor's DeepSeek moment," proving large language models can emerge outside centralized labs.
Grayscale filed an S-1 registration with the SEC on December 30, 2025, to convert its Bittensor Trust into a spot ETP under the ticker GTAO. A formal spot TAO ETF application followed in March 2026, with Bitwise also filing. SEC decision expected August 2026.

In May 2026, Bittensor implemented an emissions refactor that concentrated rewards among the top ~30 highest-performing subnets, reducing indiscriminate distribution to lower-quality nodes. This upgrade strengthens the network by rewarding real intelligence production over participation theater.

Then on June 12, 2026, the US government ordered Anthropic to shut down Fable 5 and Mythos 5 for all users worldwide. The Commerce Department cited national security concerns over a potential jailbreak vulnerability. Anthropic was forced to disable both models globally because it cannot selectively restrict access by nationality. The most powerful commercially available AI models went dark for the entire planet because of one government directive. Bittensor's thesis, that AI must be decentralized to survive, was validated in the most dramatic way possible.

🔵 Ecosystem Narrative

Bittensor's ecosystem operates as a "meta-network" of specialized AI marketplaces, each competing to produce the best intelligence for specific tasks. Bittensor brings the same type of abstraction which Ethereum added to Bitcoin for running decentralized contracts, but onto Bitcoin's inverse innovation, digital markets.

Key dynamics include:

➛ Subnets are specialized AI marketplaces, each focused on a specific task (text generation, image processing, GPU compute, data scraping, search, etc.). Currently 120+ active subnets, with plans to double to 256.

➛ Miners provide AI models and compute within subnets. Validators evaluate and rank the quality of miners' work. The best-performing models earn the most TAO rewards.

➛ Yuma Consensus (Proof of Intelligence) rewards participants based on the informational value of their contributions, not just hardware or stake. The network literally learns and adapts based on validator feedback.

➛ Dynamic TAO (dTAO) gives each subnet its own alpha token tradeable against TAO via an AMM. Emissions flow based on market-determined value, not centralized validator decisions.

➛ Emissions Refactor (May 2026) concentrated rewards among the top ~30 performing subnets, eliminating waste and rewarding genuine intelligence production over low-quality participation.

➛ Covenant-72B proved decentralized training works at scale: 72B parameters, 70+ nodes, outperforming LLaMA-2-70B, fully open-source under Apache license.

➛ EVM compatibility (2025) enables DeFi applications, liquid staking, and smart contract deployment on subnets, bridging Bittensor with the broader Web3 ecosystem.

➛ Notable subnets include Chutes (SN64, serverless AI compute with 4,000+ GPUs), Nineteen AI (SN19, decentralized LLM access), Masa Network (SN42, decentralized data scraping), and Targon by Manifold Labs (SN4, verifiable decentralized search).

⚪ Token Utilities

$TAO powers the entire decentralized AI economy:

➛ Mining & Validation Rewards - Miners earn TAO for providing quality AI outputs. Validators earn for accurately ranking miner performance. Currently 7,200 TAO minted daily (pre-first halving).

➛ Staking - Stake TAO to secure subnets, delegate to validators, and participate in the network's security model.

➛ Access to AI Services - Pay TAO to extract machine intelligence from the network, from LLM inference to GPU compute to data pipelines.

➛ Subnet Registration - Pay TAO to register new subnets, creating new AI marketplaces within the ecosystem.

➛ Governance - TAO holders participate in protocol governance through the Senate (top 12 validators by delegated TAO) and community proposals.

⚪ Key Features

➛ Censorship-Resistant AI - No kill switch. No CEO to receive a government directive. No centralized server to disable. After the Fable 5 shutdown, this isn't a feature. It's the feature.

➛ Subnet Architecture - Specialized AI marketplaces where miners compete to produce the best intelligence for specific tasks. Each subnet has its own incentive design, alpha token, and validator/miner
composition.

➛ Proof of Intelligence (Yuma Consensus) - Rewards based on the informational value of contributions. The network learns from validator feedback and adapts, ensuring outputs meet real-world demand.

➛ Bitcoin-Inspired Tokenomics - 21M max supply, no premine, halving cycles, fair launch. TAO functions as a genuine commodity powering decentralized machine intelligence.

➛ Covenant-72B - The largest language model ever trained in a decentralized manner (72B parameters, 70+ nodes, open-source). Proof that centralized labs aren't the only path to frontier AI.

➛ Dynamic TAO (dTAO) - Market-driven emission allocation where each subnet's alpha token price determines its share of rewards.

➛ EVM Compatibility - Enables smart contract deployment, DeFi integration, liquid staking, and cross-chain interoperability.

➛ Grayscale & Bitwise ETF Applications - Institutional validation with pending spot TAO ETF filings. SEC decision expected August 2026.

➛ Nvidia CEO Endorsement - Jensen Huang specifically referenced Covenant-72B and Bittensor's decentralized training approach.

🔵 Meet the Bittensor Team

Bittensor is built by AI researchers and distributed systems engineers who left positions at Google, VMware, and top universities to build the decentralized alternative to Big Tech AI.

▶️ Core Members:

➛ Jacob Robert Steeves [ @const_reborn ] - Founder | Previously worked as a machine learning engineer at Google's Google Brain team and was involved with Knowm, a company focused on neuromemristive processors. Bachelor of Applied Science in Mathematics and Computer Science from Simon Fraser University. Conceived Bittensor as early as 2015.

➛ Ala Shaabana [ @shibshib89 ] - Co-Founder | Postdoctoral Fellow at the University of Waterloo, Assistant Professor at the University of Toronto, specializing in machine learning research at FOR ai. PhD in Computer Science from McMaster University. Previously senior software engineer at Instacart and VMware.

➛ Garrett Oetken - CTO | Leads technical architecture and protocol development for the Bittensor network.

➛ Opentensor Foundation - The non-profit organization managing protocol development and governance. The project has grown to 45 core contributors distributed across North America, Europe, and Asia.

🔵 Ratings

➛ Use Case: ★★★★★ (5/5) - After June 12, 2026, Bittensor's use case rating requires no justification beyond one sentence: the US government shut down Anthropic's most powerful AI for the entire planet, and Bittensor's AI can't be shut down by anyone. But the fundamentals were already a 5 before yesterday. The only protocol that has proven decentralized AI training works at frontier scale (Covenant-72B). The subnet architecture creates an infinitely expandable marketplace for every type of AI service. Nvidia's CEO endorsed it. Grayscale filed for an ETF. 120+ active subnets. The emissions refactor concentrated rewards to the best performers. No other project comes close.

➛ Tokenomics: ★★★★★ (5/5) - Bitcoin-grade tokenomics applied to AI. 21M fixed supply, no premine, fair launch, halving cycles. TAO functions as a genuine commodity powering machine intelligence, not speculative governance tokens. The dTAO upgrade made emissions market-driven, so capital flows to the most valuable subnets naturally. ~52% of supply is circulating, meaning scarcity increases over time. The emissions refactor eliminated waste by concentrating rewards to the top 30 subnets. If Bitcoin's tokenomics are the gold standard, TAO mirrors them exactly while adding intelligent, market-driven emission allocation. This is as clean as it gets.

➛ Audits: ★★★★ (4/5) - The project has completed three third-party security audits and the entire codebase is open-source under Apache 2.0. The ecosystem is actively monitored with ongoing peer review. The 1-point deduction accounts for the March 2026 supply chain attack on Python packages (quickly disclosed and remediated), the Opentensor Foundation's Proof of Authority control over the chain, and the inherent complexity of the subnet architecture creating a larger attack surface. Security is solid and improving, but the centralization vectors and ecosystem complexity mean continued hardening is essential as the network scales.

➛ Community: ★★★★★ (5/5) - Bittensor has one of the most technically sophisticated communities in crypto. The ecosystem spans AI researchers, ML engineers, GPU miners, subnet operators, and DeFi builders. Covenant-72B was trained across 70+ decentralized nodes with no whitelist, proving genuine community participation in frontier AI. Discord is active with 12,000+ members. The subnet ecosystem is vibrant with real products generating real utility (Chutes with 4,000+ GPUs, Nineteen AI, Masa Network, Targon). The dTAO upgrade empowered the community with market-driven capital allocation. The 0.5 deduction is for governance centralization (Triumvirate of 3 Opentensor members proposes changes, Senate of 12 top validators votes), which doesn't yet match the protocol's decentralization ideals.

🔵 Conclusion

Yesterday, the United States government proved Bittensor's entire thesis in a single directive.
One order. One company. One kill switch. Fable 5 and Mythos 5 gone for the entire planet. Hundreds of millions of users lost access. Thousands of businesses disrupted. Anthropic called it a "misunderstanding." The models went dark anyway. Because when your AI runs on centralized servers, compliance isn't optional. The kill switch exists. And yesterday someone used it.

Bittensor has no kill switch. No CEO to receive a directive. No centralized server to disable. No single jurisdiction to comply with. 120+ subnets running across thousands of nodes worldwide, producing real intelligence, governed by code and token holders, not corporate boards subject to government orders.

The fundamentals were already the strongest in the AI x crypto intersection before yesterday. The only protocol that trained a 72B parameter model in a fully decentralized manner. Bitcoin-grade tokenomics. Nvidia's CEO endorsement. Grayscale and Bitwise ETF filings. Emissions refactored to reward the best subnets. 45+ core contributors. $2.3B market cap.

But after yesterday, the fundamental question has changed. It's no longer "do we need decentralized AI?" The US government just answered that for everyone. The question is now: "which decentralized AI network has actually proven it works?" And there's only one answer. Bittensor trained Covenant-72B across 70+ nodes, outperformed Meta's LLaMA-2-70B, and open-sourced it under Apache license. No other project has done anything remotely comparable.

The Commerce Department shut down the world's most powerful AI with a Friday evening email.

Bittensor's response? It kept running. It will always keep running.

That's the point. That was always the point.
